name: Oysters Souvenir De Tahaa
recipeInstructions:
Cream of Shallots Sauce:
8 ounces White wine, dry
2 ounces Lemon juice, freshly squeezed
4 ounces Heavy cream
4 ounces Oyster juice from shucked oysters
4 tablespoons Chopped shallots
Combine the wine, lemon juice, and oyster juice in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat until all liquid has evaporated.
Add cream and bring to a boil, whisking constantly. Stir in butter until melted.
Season with salt and pepper. Strain through a fine sieve into another pan and keep warm.
Assembly:
1. Beat eggs in a bowl and set aside.
2. Mix fresh herbs with bread crumbs and set aside.
3. Shuck oysters and save juice and shells.
4. Place oysters on a dry towel. Sprinkle well with flour.
5. Dip oysters into egg mixture, then the crumb/herb mixture.
6. Place oysters on a dry towel and refrigerate.
1. In a large sauté pan, heat olive oil and butter until butter sizzles.
2. Add oysters and cook 1 minute on each side until lightly browned and crispy.
3. Remove and place on paper towels to absorb excess fat.
4. Warm serving plates. Pour some sauce onto plates and place an oyster shell in the center with a garnish of parsley and half lime.
5. Arrange remaining oysters in a circular pattern around the garnish.
6. Serve immediately.
